texto: The constant talker will never, or a least rarely, grasp truth. Of course even he must experience some truths, otherwise he could not exist. He does notice certain facts, observe certain relations, draw conclusions and make plans. But he does not yet possess genuine truth, which comes into being only when the essence of an object, the significance of a relaton, and what is valid and eternal in this world reveal themselves. This requires the spacousness, freedom, and pure receptiveness of that inner “clean-swept room” whilch silence alone can create
explicacao: Orientação direta de Romano Guardini sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “The constant talker will never, or a least rarely, grasp truth. Of course even he must experience…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, silence, stillness, útil em momentos de reflexao-noturna e stillness.

texto: To know Christ entails accepting his will as norm. When we feel this we draw back, startled for it means the cross. The it is better to say honestly: “I can’t yet,” than to mouth pious phrases. Slow there with the large words “self-suffender,” and “sacrifice.” It is better to admit our weakness and ask him to teach us strength.
